records.ety: [OE. shunien, schunien, schonien, AS. scunian, sceonian; cf. D. schuinen to slepe, schuin oblique, sloping, Icel. skunda, skynda, to hasten. Cf. Schooner, Scoundrel, Shunt.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Shunned (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Shunning.]

defs: To avoid; to keep clear of; to get out of the way of; to escape from; to eschew; as, to shun rocks, shoals, vice.
quotes: I am pure from the blood of all men. For I have not shunned to declare unto you all the counsel of God.
Scarcity and want shall shun you.
